propositions: A project criterion evaluation corpus must be authored to calibrate the blocking project.portability criterion against real breakage versus harmless silence or valid customization.

The eval spec must declare fixed contract defaults: prompt plan-review-project-portability, model anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6, epochs 3, gate at_least(2), coverage_threshold 1.0, and one deterministic emits_valid_findings scorer.

The dataset must contain exactly eight cases: four must-fire (PORT-F1..F4, expect finding) with four-label notes and four must-not-fire (PORT-N1..N4, expect pass), plus a gold_set covering all eight with input and label.

Structural tests must use the real eval loader and validate_eval_spec with an injected solve function, making no billable model calls, while a live rebar criteria eval run provides operator-attested semantic calibration.

The live release threshold requires recall 1.0, false-accept 0.0, agreement 1.0, per-case stability >= 0.6666666667, and Cohen kappa >= 0.70 measured as calibrate_criterion expected-vs-observed fire/no-fire agreement.

Calibration adjustments may only modify the project rubric prompt; no changes may touch generic registry, verifier, disposition, or src/rebar implementation, and failed/partial live runs are retried at most three times before recording failure.
